name: Snowflake
shortDescription: The AI Data Cloud — a multi-cloud platform unifying data warehousing, data lakes, and AI workloads across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
businessModel: Consumption-based model (pay for compute + storage, not seats). FY2026 product revenue $4.72B (+29% YoY), NRR 126%. 779 customers >$1M ARR.
marketContext: Enterprise cloud data infrastructure is consolidating around AI workloads. Snowflake sits as a neutral layer above the hyperscalers, competing with Databricks on the open-source side and AWS/GCP/Azure native tools on the lock-in side.
notes:
TRADING: $255.55 | Mkt Cap $88.2B | EV $86.9B | 14.9x EV/FY27E rev | 48 analysts, strong_buy
COMPS: Databricks ~25x (pvt), Palantir 41x, Datadog 17x

brief.businessOverview: Snowflake operates the AI Data Cloud — a fully managed, multi-cloud platform that unifies data warehousing, data lakes, data engineering, and AI workloads across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. Founded in 2012, headquartered in Bozeman, MT, ~11,100 employees. Core value proposition is cloud neutrality: it sits above all three hyperscalers, allowing enterprises to store, share, and query data across clouds without copying it — something AWS, Azure, and GCP structurally cannot offer without undermining their own lock-in strategies.
brief.productModel: Snowflake runs an almost entirely consumption-based model — customers pay for compute credits and storage used, not fixed seats. This creates a natural land-and-expand motion. FY2026 product revenue was $4.72B (+29% YoY). Key products: Data Cloud (core warehousing), Cortex AI (LLM inference), Snowflake Intelligence (agentic AI for business users), Snowpark (data engineering + ML), Snowflake Native Apps, Streamlit. 779 customers generate >$1M in trailing product revenue.

recentNews.headline: Q1 FY2027: Product revenue +34% YoY to $1.33B, EPS $0.39 vs. $0.14 estimate (+179%)
recentNews.whyItMatters: Accelerating growth and massive EPS beat signal AI monetization is inflecting — removes the key bear thesis around decelerating consumption.

recentNews.headline: $6B multi-year strategic collaboration agreement with AWS signed
recentNews.whyItMatters: Deepens hyperscaler alignment and signals long-term revenue visibility while reducing competitive risk from AWS's own data products.

recentNews.headline: Full-year product revenue guidance raised to $5.84B (31% growth)
recentNews.whyItMatters: Guidance raise following accelerating growth reduces near-term execution risk and provides a positive catalyst for multiple expansion.

recentNews.headline: Natoma acquisition — enterprise Model Context Protocol platform for AI agents
recentNews.whyItMatters: Bolsters agentic AI governance capabilities, addressing enterprise concerns about rogue AI agents.

recentNews.headline: HSBC upgrades to Buy; Citi, Piper Sandler, Raymond James, Truist raise price targets
recentNews.whyItMatters: Broad post-earnings upgrades reflect renewed institutional conviction.

recentNews.headline: Jonathan Beaulier appointed CRO, replacing Mike Gannon
recentNews.whyItMatters: CRO transition during AI ramp introduces near-term GTM execution risk — watch over next 2 quarters.

recentNews.headline: $200M multi-year OpenAI partnership for native model access within Cortex AI
recentNews.whyItMatters: Reinforces Snowflake as neutral, model-agnostic enterprise AI data platform vs. Databricks and Microsoft Fabric.

earningsTakeaways.aiCommentary: Management positioned Snowflake as the 'agentic control plane for the enterprise.' Snowflake Intelligence scaled to 2,500 active accounts in 3 months; 9,100+ accounts use AI products. CEO Ramaswamy: AI is driving direct consumption and second-order effects — faster migrations, easier pipelines, simpler agent deployment.
earningsTakeaways.demandCommentary: Product revenue re-accelerated from 30% in Q4 FY2026 to 34% in Q1 FY2027 — strongest sequential dollar growth in company history. 13,912 total customers, 616 net new in quarter (+38% YoY). NRR expanded to 126%.

keyRisks: Valuation: ~120x non-GAAP P/E keeps buy-side cautious even at 34% growth; 6 of 51 analysts still at Hold/Sell
New CRO risk: Jonathan Beaulier appointed March 2026 during critical AI ramp — watch GTM execution and rep retention over next 2 quarters
Databricks competition: growing at 65% vs. SNOW's 34%, pushing into core SQL/warehouse territory; harder to track as a private company
Consumption model volatility: revenue can decelerate rapidly if enterprises optimize workloads or reduce cloud spend
Margin expansion pace: some investors focused on whether margin expansion keeps pace with the raised revenue outlook

slideBullets: Snowflake's consumption-based AI Data Cloud accelerated to 34% product revenue growth in Q1 FY2027 — the company's strongest sequential dollar growth in history — driven by rapid adoption of Cortex AI and Snowflake Intelligence across 9,100+ enterprise accounts

A $6B multi-year AWS partnership and $200M OpenAI deal cement Snowflake's position as the neutral, model-agnostic enterprise AI data platform, structurally differentiating it from hyperscaler-native alternatives
NRR of 126% and RPO of $9.2B reflect durable land-and-expand economics, with 779 customers generating >$1M in annual product revenue — a cohort expanding rapidly as AI workloads consume more compute
Management raised full-year product revenue guidance to $5.84B (31% growth) and targets 13.5% non-GAAP operating margin, signaling confidence in both demand pipeline and path to profitability

At 14.9x EV/FY2027E revenue, Snowflake trades at a significant discount to high-growth AI peers like Databricks (~25x) and Palantir (41x), despite re-accelerating growth and a broadening AI product portfolio
diligenceQuestions: Databricks displacement risk: In deals where both are evaluated, what is Snowflake's current win rate and how has it trended over the last 4 quarters? What workloads are they losing most frequently?

AI consumption durability: Of the 9,100+ accounts using AI products, what percentage have moved beyond pilot to production workloads generating meaningful consumption revenue — and what does the ramp curve look like from activation to $100K+ spend?
CRO transition impact: What is the pipeline coverage ratio and average sales cycle for $1M+ deals heading into Q2, given the CRO change? Has there been any rep attrition in enterprise accounts?
